cards.title: What the Stars Know
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@LUNA
~Every pinprick light above this town
~Is a question no one's written down
~What does Jupiter know that we do not?
~What does Venus feel and have forgot?

[Chorus]
@LUNA
~What the stars know, I am learning slow
~What the dark holds, I am learning to hold too
~Somewhere in the turning of this sky
~Is the answer to the question: why?

@CHORUS
~What the stars know, she is learning slow
~Reading patterns in the infinite
~Millhaven, look up — there is more than you can see
~More than you can bear to know

[Verse 2]
@LUNA
~My father's telescope is pointing west
~He taught me Orion, taught me all the rest
~He said the sky is patient, doesn't rush
~It's been here long before us, and will hush

(to herself)
~Long after us

[Final Chorus]
@LUNA & CHORUS
~What the stars know, we are learning slow
~What the dark holds, we are learning to hold too
~Somewhere in the turning of this sky
~Is the reason I keep watching — that's why

cards.title: Oscar smashes the telescope
cards.note: Luna discovers their late father's telescope is broken. Oscar says it's time to move on — the observatory is being sold.

cards.title: The eviction notice
cards.note: Mayor Hatch's notice arrives: the observatory closes in 30 days. Dr. Vale has nowhere to go.

cards.title: Millhaven Wakes
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@CHORUS
~Millhaven wakes at half past six
~Coffee on the stove
~Millhaven counts its little tricks
~Everything we know

~Hardware store opens up at seven
~Baker's rolling dough
~Kids are walking up to heaven
~That's the hill, you know

[Mayor's Section]
@MAYOR HATCH
~Ladies and gentlemen of Millhaven
~I want to talk about our future
~Growth and progress, a better day
~A luxury resort hotel and spa —

@CHORUS
~Oh
(pause)
~Oh

@MAYOR HATCH
~On the site of the old observatory
~Which has sat dark and empty
~For the better part of three years
~And frankly is an eyesore

@CHORUS
~Millhaven nods, Millhaven sighs
~Millhaven shrugs and looks away
~Growth and progress, no surprise
~It's the same as yesterday

cards.title: Luna rallies Felix
cards.note: Felix is terrified of the observatory at night. Luna talks him into helping anyway.

cards.title: Fix the Broken Thing
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@LUNA
~The telescope's in pieces on the floor
~My brother says we shouldn't need it anymore
~But I can feel the weight of every part
~This broken thing is everything we are

[Pre-Chorus]
~There's a right way and a wrong way to let go
~There's a fight I haven't started yet, I know
~There's a version of this story where I walk away
~But that's not the version I can live with today

[Chorus]
@LUNA
~Fix the broken thing
~Don't leave it on the shelf
~Fix the broken thing
~You can't save it by yourself
~Every shattered piece
~Is asking to be whole
~Fix the broken thing
~Because fixing it fixes you

[Bridge]
~Dad, I'm trying
~I know you'd say the glass was just the glass
~I know you'd say the frame was just the frame
~But everything you showed me through that lens
~I'm not ready to let it be past tense

cards.title: Meeting Dr. Vale
cards.note: Luna and Felix break in. Dr. Vale is packing up her research. She is cold and dismissive.

cards.title: Equations
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@DR. VALE
~F equals MA and the mass of the earth
~Is six times ten to the twenty-fourth
~If you want to know your weight on Mars
~Divide by point three eight

~You think that's beautiful? I find it plain.
~The mystery was always what remained
~The parts we couldn't solve, the parts we'd miss
~The anomaly. The residual. This.

[Chorus]
@DR. VALE
~Equations, equations
~Twelve thousand lines of notes
~And somewhere in the middle of these pages
~Is a thing that no one's seen
~Equations, equations
~The universe keeps score
~And what it's scored so far
~Is so much more than I'd bargained for

[Verse 2]
~I've spent forty years alone in this glass dome
~Talking to the sky like it was home
~Some people said I should have found a life
~A husband or a wife or social skills

~But Jupiter was good company, you know
~And Saturn's rings put on a lovely show
~And forty years of data in these charts
~Is forty years of practicing the art

cards.title: The deal
cards.note: Dr. Vale agrees to help Luna fix the telescope if Luna helps her digitize 40 years of star charts before the closing.

cards.title: Progress (The Mayor's Vision)
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@MAYOR HATCH
~I didn't get elected to stand still
~I didn't get elected to look back
~I got elected on a promise, on a vision
~Now let me tell you what this town can have

[Chorus]
@MAYOR HATCH
~Progress! That's the word I'm selling
~Progress! It's a better story
~Than a telescope that's sat dark
~For the last three years
~Progress! Signs and construction
~Jobs for the taking
~I am the man who sees what this town could be
~And it starts with tearing down what's in our way

[Verse 2]
@MAYOR HATCH
~Now I know some people have their feelings
~Some people have their nostalgia and their grief
~But feelings don't create a tax base
~And grief don't bring a single job relief

@COUNCIL
~That's right

[Bridge]
@MAYOR HATCH
~You want a future? I'm your future
~You want a vision? Here it is:
~New money coming into Millhaven
~This whole tired town is mine to give

cards.title: Oscar sides with the Mayor
cards.note: Oscar publicly supports the sale at the town meeting. Luna is blindsided.

cards.title: Dark
First glimpse
cards.note: The telescope is repaired just enough to show one star. Luna names it after her father.

cards.title: Paper Planets
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@DR. VALE
~I used to name them after scientists
~Kepler this and Herschel that
~But your father broke the pattern, didn't he
~Named one Rosie, for your cat

@LUNA
~For my mom's cat. Before I was born.

@DR. VALE
~He said she was the only one
~Who sat with him through the cold nights
~Didn't complain. Just purred.
~I thought that was very funny.

[Chorus]
@LUNA & DR. VALE
~Paper planets, paper maps
~Everything we know on charts and scraps
~Forty years of looking at the dark
~And we're still just at the start

~Paper planets, careful hands
~Things that no one's named, no one understands
~Your father's charts are still in here somewhere
~We'll find him in the data if we dare

[Verse 2]
@LUNA
~You knew my dad?
@DR. VALE
~He volunteered here every summer
~Nineteen eighty-two through eighty-six
~He wasn't a professional, just curious
~The curious ones are usually the fix

@LUNA
~He never told me that.

[Outro]
@DR. VALE
~Your father was the one who first noticed
~The anomaly in chart seventeen
~He marked it in the margin: investigate
~I never got around to what he meant

cards.title: Oscar's secret
cards.note: Felix discovers Oscar took money from Hatch. Oscar didn't want Luna to know they're broke.

cards.title: What He Left Us
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@OSCAR
~I was seventeen when Dad got sick
~You were six and didn't know the trick
~Of carrying the whole thing on your face
~Like a fact — not something to erase

~I learned it from him, he learned from his dad
~The Carter men don't show you when it's bad
~We'll fix the thing before you know it's broke
~We'll carry the water and we'll laugh at the joke

[Chorus]
@OSCAR
~What he left us is a sky full of questions
~What he left us is a lens and a light
~What he left us we can't put back together
~But we'll carry it between us through the night

[Verse 2]
@LUNA
~You sold us out to Hatch for thirty thousand
@OSCAR
~I sold us out because we're thirty thousand under
~The mortgage is in collections, Luna
~Dad's insurance didn't cover everything

@LUNA
~You should have told me

@OSCAR
~You're seventeen

@LUNA
~So what?

[Chorus]
@LUNA & OSCAR
~What he left us is a sky full of questions
~What he left us is a lens and a light
~What he left us we can't put back together
~But we'll carry it between us through the night

[Bridge]
@OSCAR
~He would have found a way. He always did.
~I'm not him, Luna. I'm just me.
@LUNA
~That's enough.
(quietly)
~That's enough.

cards.title: The petition
cards.note: Luna starts a petition to landmark the observatory. The town is divided.

cards.title: Everybody Sees Something
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@LUNA
~I drew a map of every star above this town
~I drew it in the margin of my notebook, upside down
~So when I hold it to the window, to the light
~It looks like Millhaven reflected in the night

[Verse 2]
@FELIX
~I thought I hated telescopes and cold
~I thought the dark was something I could never hold
~But Luna showed me Orion and his belt
~And I felt something I'd never let myself felt

[Chorus]
@LUNA, FELIX, CHORUS
~Everybody sees something in the sky
~Some see God or chaos, some just try
~To find the shape that matches what they know
~The thing that's theirs alone — the thing that glows

~Everybody carries something in the dark
~A question or a compass or a spark
~And Millhaven has been looking at its feet
~While the whole universe was right there — incomplete

[Bridge]
@CHORUS
~We signed the petition
~We're not sure what it means
~But something's happening

@LUNA
~It means we're saying this matters
~It means we're saying we see it
~And you can't sell what we've already named

[Final Chorus]
@ALL
~Everybody sees something in the sky
~Tonight the sky is ours, and here is why:
~Thirty names on paper, thirty people who looked up
~Millhaven, thirty people dared to look up

cards.title: Hatch pulls the permit
cards.note: Hatch uses an obscure zoning rule to accelerate the demolition to 10 days.

cards.title: Ten Days
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@LUNA
~He's moved the deadline to ten days
~Ten days from now the wrecking ball
~Ten days before the data's gone
~Ten days, and no one comes at all

[Company Section]
@DR. VALE
~Ten days to pack a life's work into boxes
@FELIX
~Ten days for Luna to fix the telescope
@OSCAR
~Ten days before I have to live with this

[Chorus]
@COMPANY
~Ten days
~Ten days
~The clock is running, the ground is shaking
~Ten days
~Ten days
~And everything we've built is breaking
~Ten days in October
~Cold enough to see your breath
~Ten days to save the thing we love
~Or ten days till its death

[Bridge]
@LUNA
~I didn't come this far to walk away
~I didn't fix that lens to lose today
~Dad used to say the sky is patient
~The sky can wait

(quietly)
~But we cannot.

[Final Chorus]
@COMPANY
~Ten days
~Ten days
~The clock is running, the ground is shaking
~Ten days
~Ten days
~And everything we've built is breaking
~Ten days, and we're still here
~Ten days, and we're still here

cards.title: Dr. Vale's discovery
cards.note: Buried in a 1987 star chart, Dr. Vale finds an anomaly — an uncatalogued object that could be a new comet.

cards.title: Something No One's Named
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@DR. VALE
~Look at this.
@LUNA
~What is it?
@DR. VALE
~Chart seventeen. October, 1987.
~Your father's handwriting in the margin.
~Here.

@LUNA
~"Something no one's named."

@DR. VALE
~He wrote those exact words.

[Verse 2]
@DR. VALE
~I ran the coordinates against the database.
~Nothing. No match. No record.
@LUNA
~What does that mean?

@DR. VALE
~It means your father saw it first.
~It means we saw it second.
~It means it's been there for thirty-nine years
~Waiting for someone to look back.

[Chorus]
@DR. VALE & LUNA
~Something no one's named
~Something no one's claimed
~Thirty-nine years in the margin of a chart
~Something no one's named
~Something waiting, patient
~To be seen by someone with a willing heart

~This is what it's for
~Every cold night, every hour
~Every numb hand, every frozen floor
~Something no one's named
~Is about to have a name

[Bridge]
@LUNA
~We have ten days.
@DR. VALE
~We have ten days.
@BOTH
~But tonight, we have this.

cards.title: Felix faces the dark
cards.note: To photograph the comet they need to climb to the dome roof at 2am. Felix has to choose.

cards.title: Braver Than I Feel
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@FELIX
~I'm afraid of the dark, that's the plain truth
~Been afraid since I was small, since my youth
~My therapist says name it, so I name it:
~Dark. Dark dark dark dark dark.

~And the dark said: good. Now come inside.

[Verse 2]
~Luna needs me on that roof at two a.m.
~Luna needs me to stop saying when-when-when
~And start saying now, saying yes, saying here I am
~Even if my knees are going to shake like mad

[Chorus]
@FELIX
~I'm braver than I feel right now
~I'm steadier than I appear
~Somewhere underneath this panic is a person
~Who is going to climb that roof tonight
~I'm braver than I feel right now
~I've been scared my whole life
~That's how I know I'm brave

[Bridge]
~My dad was scared of flying, flew a thousand times
~My mom was scared of singing, sang me into sleep
~Bravery is not the lack of fear, they said
~It's the doing of the thing while scared

[Final Chorus]
@FELIX
~I'm braver than I feel right now
~I'm going up the ladder in the dark
~And somewhere in the cold, in the vertical, the air
~I'm going to find out who I are

(spoken)
Are. Am. Whatever.
~I'm going.

cards.title: The photograph
cards.note: They get the image. Dr. Vale emails it to the university. Now they wait.

cards.title: What He Left Us (Reprise)
The university responds
cards.note: The comet is real. It will be named Vale-Carter after Dr. Vale and Luna's father. The press wants to visit the observatory.

cards.title: Hatch doubles down
cards.note: Mayor Hatch gets an injunction. The observatory stays closed for the press visit. All seems lost.

cards.title: Rooftop (11 O'Clock)
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@DR. VALE
~I have spent forty years in a glass dome
~Talking to a universe that doesn't answer back
~Or — no. That isn't right.
~It answers. Just not out loud.

~It answers in the shape of things
~In the way a comet curves
~In a margin note from 1987
~In a girl who breaks in through my back door

[Verse 2]
~I have a letter from the university
~They'd like to name the comet Vale-Carter
~They'd like to send a delegation
~But the building's under injunction

~My whole life is under injunction.

[Chorus]
@DR. VALE
~This rooftop
~This roof of the world I've built
~This rooftop
~Where I've kept all my guilt
~And all my grief
~And all my forty years of proof
~That the sky is enough
~That the dark is enough
~That a roof is enough if the stars are above it

~But now there's a girl on my rooftop
~And a boy who's afraid of the dark
~And a town down below who might see it
~If I'll just let the doors blow apart

[Bridge]
~I have been a closed door my entire life
~Locked and painted and lovely from the outside
~Luna, you broke in.
~Don't stop.

[Final Chorus]
@DR. VALE
~This rooftop
~Is bigger than me
~This rooftop
~Is what forty years can be
~When you share it
~When you open it
~When you stand on it and say:
~Come up. Come in. Come see.

cards.title: The town shows up
cards.note: Townspeople arrive with blankets and lawn chairs to watch the comet from the observatory grounds, injunction or not.

cards.title: Oscar concedes
cards.note: Oscar takes the broken telescope to the roof and fixes the last piece himself. He hands it to Luna.

cards.title: Hatch backs down
cards.note: With cameras and half the town watching, Mayor Hatch withdraws the demolition order.

cards.title: Bright Side of the Moon
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@LUNA
~My father used to wake me up at midnight
~Bundle me in blankets, take me to the roof
~He'd say: the moon is at its best at midnight
~Because the shadow shows you what's true

~The craters and the ridges, the dark valleys
~The texture that you'd miss in daylight's glare
~He said: the bright side of the moon is beautiful
~But the bright side hides the interesting parts

[Chorus]
@LUNA
~The bright side of the moon
~Is what the whole world sees
~But the far side, the dark side
~Is where the answers live
~The bright side of the moon
~Is where the story starts
~But the dark side is where we go
~When we're brave enough to go

@COMPANY
~When we're brave enough to go

[Verse 2]
@DR. VALE
~I have a name now, a comet with my name
~Vale-Carter, one word, the sky and the same
~Forty years of looking and I'm finally found
~By a girl from Millhaven on the wrong side of town

@OSCAR
~I thought I was protecting you from all of this
~Turns out I was protecting me
~From asking for help, from falling apart
~From being a Carter who can't fix a thing

[Final Chorus]
@COMPANY
~The bright side of the moon
~Is what the whole world sees
~But Millhaven learned to look at the dark
~And we found ourselves
~We found a comet and a girl and a name
~And the far side
~The far side is beautiful too

@LUNA
~Dad — we found it.
~We found it.

LUNA.desc: 17, a dreamer who maps the stars from her rooftop

OSCAR.desc: Luna's older brother, a skeptic with a broken telescope

DR. VALE.desc: The local astronomer, brilliant and lonely

FELIX.desc: Luna's best friend, afraid of the dark

MAYOR HATCH.desc: 55, wants to sell the observatory land to developers

titlePage.subtitle: A new musical
titlePage.authors: Book, Music & Lyrics by C. Carter
titlePage.productionNotes:
BRIGHT SIDE OF THE MOON requires a unit set that can transform fluidly between the observatory interior, the town square, and the rooftop. Projections are essential — the night sky should be a living character throughout. The chorus should always be visible, even in intimate scenes, seated at the edges of the stage as observers.

All songs should be performed without amplification where possible. The score is written for an eight-piece pit orchestra.
titlePage.acknowledgements: This musical was developed at the Sundance Theatre Lab and the O'Neill Music Theater Conference. Grateful thanks to the original workshop cast, the Millhaven Children's Planetarium for use of their facilities, and to every kid who ever stayed up too late looking at the sky.
